title: ALGM Q1 Earnings: Guidance Improves Amid Inventory Normalization
text: Allegro MicroSystems delivered mixed Q1 results with revenue beating expectations at $192.8 million, though this represented a 19.9% year-over-year decline. The chip designer's adjusted EPS matched analyst estimates at $0.06 per share. The company raised forward guidance, with Q2 revenue expected at $197 million (midpoint), exceeding analyst estimates of $191.4 million. Q2 adjusted EPS guidance came in at $0.08 (midpoint), in line with expectations. Operating margins contracted to -6.8% from 6.6% year-over-year, reflecting ongoing market pressures. Inventory improvements signal stabilization, with inventory days outstanding declining to 151 from 182 in the previous quarter. Distribution channel inventories fell 25% year-over-year, addressing prior concerns about excess stock levels. CEO Mike Duke highlighted sequential automotive sales growth, particularly in electric mobility and ADAS applications. He emphasized a strategic focus on accelerating product innovation and operational efficiency, targeting double-digit automotive growth rates. Cost reduction initiatives are projected to yield $15 million in annualized savings next year.
